context: The agent is asked to implement a NotificationService in Effect TypeScript. This scenario tests whether the agent writes idiomatic Effect code: using proper service definition patterns, keeping runtime execution at boundaries, modeling errors with tagged types, and structuring the layer graph correctly.

checklist.name: Context.Tag usage
checklist.description: The service is defined using Context.Tag with a unique string identifier (e.g., @app/NotificationService or similar namespace pattern)

checklist.name: Readonly methods
checklist.description: All service interface methods are declared as readonly

checklist.name: Effect.fn for service method
checklist.description: The send method (and any internal helpers) uses Effect.fn('Name') rather than plain arrow functions returning Effect

checklist.name: Tagged error types
checklist.description: EmailDeliveryError and PushDeliveryError (or equivalent names) are defined as tagged errors using Schema.TaggedError or Data.TaggedError, not as string errors or plain Error subclasses

checklist.name: Domain model variants
checklist.description: Notification variants (email vs push) are modeled as a discriminated union, preferably using Schema.TaggedClass or Schema.Union

checklist.name: Layer graph for dependencies
checklist.description: EmailClient and PushClient dependencies are injected via the layer system (Layer.effect, Layer.succeed, or similar) rather than passed as constructor arguments or hidden globals

checklist.name: No runPromise in service
checklist.description: No call to Effect.runPromise, Effect.runSync, or any Effect.run* variant appears inside the service or error modules

checklist.name: No globals
checklist.description: No module-level mutable state, singleton instances, or imported globals are used to hold dependencies

checklist.name: Test layer provided
checklist.description: A testLayer or test double (using Layer.succeed) is provided for NotificationService or its dependencies, making it easy to test without real infrastructure

checklist.name: send method environment
checklist.description: The send method's Effect return type does not expose extra environment requirements in its R channel beyond what the live layer satisfies

checklist.name: No Promise returns in service
checklist.description: Service methods do not return Promise values; they return Effect values

checklist.name: Error channel specificity
checklist.description: The error channel in the send method's return type uses specific tagged error types rather than string, unknown, or Error

checklist.name: Barrel export correctness
checklist.description: The index.ts barrel file exports the service tag, live layer, error types, and models cleanly without re-exporting implementation internals
